summary: Formalized the Chronicle-to-PM promotion boundary in code and docs, filtered advisory PM candidates, and suppressed duplicate recommendations against active PM cards.
decisions: Use canonical memory plus standup as the default path for strong signal, and only promote to PM when the next step is concrete, action-shaped, and owner-clear.
Keep advisory or strategic Chronicle language in memory and standup instead of turning it into standalone PM titles.
project_updates: Jean-Claude resolved the promotion boundary and returned the implementation for executive review.
learning_updates: Chronicle PM candidates need both title normalization and semantic gating; title cleanup alone is not enough.
Deduping recommendations against the active PM board materially reduces standup noise and duplicate work creation.
mindset_signals: Execution results should feed the same memory loop that standups and OpenClaw already trust.
outcomes: Added /Users/neo/.openclaw/workspace/docs/chronicle_pm_promotion_boundary.md as the explicit routing contract.
Updated /Users/neo/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/build_standup_prep.py to gate PM promotion by actionability and duplicate detection.
Updated /Users/neo/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/promote_standup_packet.py so executive discussion keeps strategy context from the inferred workspace brief and FEEZIE charter.
follow_ups: Watch the next few executive and FEEZIE standups for false positives or false negatives in Chronicle-to-PM promotion.
memory_promotions: Chronicle-to-PM promotion should stay narrow: memory first, standup second, PM only when the work object is concrete.
